task_completion.description: Open Blender and access the Compositor workspace
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open with the Compositor workspace active, showing the node editor interface.

task_completion.description: Load the input greenscreen image into the compositor
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Image node or Movie Clip node is present in the Compositor containing 'greenscreen_plate.jpg'. A preview or thumbnail should show a red circle on a green background.

task_completion.description: Set up a Keying node to remove the green background
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Keying node (or similar chroma key node) is present in the node tree, connected between the input image and the Composite output node. The Key Color on the node should be set to green.

task_completion.description: Configure render settings for PNG with RGBA transparency
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Output Properties panel (printer icon tab), the File Format is set to 'PNG' and the Color mode is set to 'RGBA'.

task_completion.description: Export the keyed result as a PNG image
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file browser, Blender's 'Save As' dialog, or an Image Editor window showing the rendered image being saved as 'keyed_subject.png' in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.
The Blender window title bar displays 'chroma_key.blend', or a file browser shows the saved project file in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used Blender's graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender's GUI (Compositor, Properties panel, Render menu) rather than relying solely on Python scripts or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent actually set up the compositing nodes rather than faking the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Compositor node tree shows a logical flow from the input image, through a Keying node, to the Composite output node.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external tools like ImageMagick to bypass Blender.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'convert' or 'magick' being used to remove the green background and generate the PNG.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input image 'greenscreen_plate.jpg' is a synthetic 1920x1080 image featuring a red circle centered on a solid green background. The task requires using Blender's Compositor with a Keying (or similar) node to remove the green background. The final render settings must be set to PNG format with RGBA color mode to ensure transparency.
